regime_justification: USDJPY pinned at 160.30 (barely below prior intervention watch line of 160.74), BOJ just raised +25bp to 1.00% but yen failed to rally, US-Iran deal easing energy risk short-term, yet carry spread still ~185bps and vol remains subdued—mixed signals, intervention risk elevated

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY at 160.30, MOF historically intervenes ~160-161, BOJ raised rates to 1% yet yen weak, vol low but pair at watch line; no rate-checks yet but policy response delivered without FX follow-through signals fragility

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ raised 25bp to 1% as expected, yet USDJPY unmoved at 160.30; carry intact (185bps spread), vol low (7-8%), Iran deal easing energy pressure—but pair at MOF watch line and failed rally signals policy inefficacy; intervention risk elevated

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Iran deal signing June 19 could further ease oil pressure, but carry mechanics intact and yen unresponsive to BOJ tightening; if USDJPY pushes >160.7 or accelerates, MOF action becomes likely; FOMC June 18 under new Chair Warsh adds uncertainty

3w.label: Likely
3w.driver: If Iran deal holds, energy pressure fades but carry spread remains wide; BOJ signaled continued hikes (next likely Q4) yet yen failed to respond to June hike; market testing MOF resolve at 160+ increases probability of intervention or sharp vol spike

4w.label: Watchful
4w.driver: By week 4, either MOF intervenes and resets (moving back to stage 1-2), or Iran deal fully de-escalates energy risk and BOJ credibility rebuilds; if neither, setup remains fragile but catalyst-dependent; scenario-weighted grade reflects path divergence

bottom_line: BOJ raised 25bp to 1% today but USDJPY stuck at 160.30—yen's failure to rally despite policy normalization signals carry mechanics trump central bank action. Spread still 185bps, vol subdued ~8%, Iran deal easing oil pressure short-term. We sit at Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention): pair at historical MOF watch line, policy delivered but ineffective, intervention risk elevated. Grades C/C/B/C reflect fragile setup—not yet unwinding but one failed MOF jawbone or USDJPY break above 160.7 could trigger Stage 3. US CPI at 4.2% (3-year high) and FOMC meeting June 18 under new Chair Warsh add cross-currents. Crypto bid (BTC +1.3% to $66.2k, XRP +12.6% to $1.28) tracking Iran risk-off relief, not carry unwind. Watch: MOF verbal escalation, USDJPY pace >1% in 2 days, MOVE spike, or HY spread widening.

trigger_map.note: USDJPY at 160.30, within historical intervention zone (160-161); no rate-checks yet but Finance Minister rhetoric in March warned of action; pair at watch line, MOF testing market patience
BOJ delivered expected +25bp to 1% today; yen failed to rally—policy inefficacy exposed; next hike likely Q4 2026 (market pricing 1.25% by year-end), but gradualism limits shock potential

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: JGB 10Y at 2.64%, up from 1-month low; prior cycle high (2008) ~1.7%; distant but normalizing; if accelerates could signal unwind of BOJ QE overhang

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: Iran deal signing June 19 eases Hormuz risk; WTI $80.41, ¥/bbl ~¥12,890; down from peaks (~¥18,000+ when WTI was $117 and USDJPY 160); import inflation pressure cooling short-term

trigger_map.trigger: USDJPY 1M risk reversals flipping to JPY-call premium
trigger_map.note: Data unavailable but low implied vol (~8%) suggests no JPY-call premium yet; if pair breaks 160.7 and vol spikes, reversal skew would flip quickly signaling intervention fear

narrative.p1: BOJ delivered the widely expected 25bp hike to 1.00% (first time since 1995) overnight, yet USDJPY barely moved—stuck at 160.30, just below the 52-week high of 160.74 and within the historical MOF intervention watch zone (160-161). The yen's failure to rally despite policy normalization exposes the dominance of carry mechanics: the US-JP 10Y spread remains at 183bps, and implied vol is subdued at ~8%. Overnight the US and Iran announced a peace deal framework (signing June 19 in Switzerland), sending WTI down ~5% to $80.41 and easing Japan's imported inflation pressure (¥/bbl down to ~¥12,890 from peaks near ¥18,000). Nikkei surged +5% on the twin tailwinds of geopolitical de-escalation and BOJ policy credibility, but USDJPY's failure to respond to the hike is the headline story. US CPI printed 4.2% (3-year high) on June 10, pushing Fed hike probability for December to 67% and keeping Treasury yields elevated (US 10Y 4.47%). Stage moved from 1 (Pressure Building) to 2 (Pre-Intervention): BOJ acted, pair at watch line, policy ineffective so far, MOF rhetoric from March still hanging over the market.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP spread 183bps (down 6bp as JGB rose 6bp, UST +0.4bp), carry-to-vol proxy ~23 (183/8), elevated vs history. USDJPY 5-day change flat, 60-day mean ~158, so pair stretched 2.3 yen above mean—compressed near the top of the range but not accelerating (MOF watches **pace**, not just level). ¥/bbl crude = 80.41×160.30 = ¥12,890, down sharply from recent peaks as Iran deal eases energy channel. We sit at **Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention)**: USDJPY at MOF watch line, BOJ policy delivered but yen unresponsive, vol low (complacency), no rate-checks yet but setup fragile. 1-week and 2-week grades both **C (Watchful)**: BOJ hike was the catalyst that should have triggered yen strength; instead carry mechanics and Fed hawkishness (CPI 4.2%, new Chair Warsh's first FOMC June 18) are overwhelming BOJ tightening. The Iran deal signing June 19 could further de-risk energy/inflation, but the yen's failure to respond to a 25bp hike is a red flag. Applied the unwind prior as a tiebreaker for 3-week grade (**B, Likely**): if USDJPY pushes above 160.7 or accelerates in pace (>1% in 2 days), MOF will likely intervene or vol will spike. Data is genuinely mixed (BOJ hawkish, energy easing, but carry intact and policy ineffective), so the prior nudges 3w from C to B. 4-week grade back to **C (Watchful)** as scenario-weighted: either MOF intervenes and resets the regime, or Iran deal + BOJ credibility rebuild and we stabilize, or we drift into Stage 3 if neither occurs.
narrative.p3: Crypto liquidity is **improving, not draining**—BTC +1.3% to $66,217, XRP +12.6% to $1.28 on strong risk-on flows post-Iran deal. XRP's double-digit surge signals alt rotation and short squeeze, not carry unwind sympathy. BTC 7-day +4.4%, tracking geopolitical de-escalation and equity strength (Nikkei +5%, S&P futures bid). No sign of crypto draining as a carry unwind symptom; instead, risk appetite is back as Hormuz reopening eases energy/inflation fears. Open interest data unavailable but the price action (BTC holding $60k support, alts rallying) suggests positioning is constructive. For the operator's crypto book, this is a **relief rally, not an all-clear**—yen carry mechanics remain intact (spread 183bps, vol 8%, USDJPY at 160.30), and the BOJ's policy inefficacy is a medium-term risk. If USDJPY breaks 160.7 or MOF intervenes, expect a sharp vol spike that could drain risk assets including crypto. Until then, crypto is riding the Iran deal tailwind; watch for decoupling if FX stress resurfaces within 1-2 weeks.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1-week: BOJ delivered +25bp to 1%, yet USDJPY unmoved at 160.30—policy inefficacy is the story. Carry spread 183bps, vol 8%, pair at MOF watch line. Iran deal signing June 19 eases energy pressure short-term, but yen's failure to rally is a red flag. Grade C (Watchful): setup fragile, intervention risk elevated, but no catalyst yet. 2-week: Iran deal could further de-risk (Hormuz reopens, oil stabilizes), but FOMC June 18 under new Chair Warsh adds uncertainty (US CPI 4.2%, hike talk). If USDJPY accelerates or breaks 160.7, MOF verbal intervention or action likely. Grade C (Watchful): mixed signals, Iran deal vs carry mechanics, watch for MOF escalation or pace pickup.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: By week 3, either the Iran deal fully de-escalates energy risk and BOJ credibility rebuilds (stabilizing yen), or USDJPY tests MOF resolve above 160.7 and vol spikes. BOJ signaled further hikes (consensus 1.25% by year-end, likely Q4), but today's hike failed to move the pair—market is testing whether policy can trump carry. Applied unwind prior here as tiebreaker: data genuinely ambiguous (energy easing, BOJ hiking, but carry intact and FX unresponsive), so nudge from C to B. Grade B (Likely): if MOF forced to intervene or USDJPY pace accelerates, early unwind signatures (vol spike, Nikkei selloff, HY widening) become probable within 3-week window.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: By week 4, regime likely resolves one of three ways: (1) MOF intervenes, vol spikes, stage moves to 3-4 then resets, (2) Iran deal + BOJ credibility + stable Fed policy allow yen to stabilize, spread compresses, back to stage 1, or (3) status quo persists (carry on, yen weak, vol low) and we drift. Scenario-weighted, most paths lead back to C: either intervention resets and we stabilize, or de-escalation allows gradual normalization. The B grade in week 3 assumes heightened catalyst risk (MOF testing, FOMC uncertainty); by week 4 that either resolves or fades. Grade C (Watchful): directional but scenario-dependent.

watch_list: MOF verbal intervention or rate-check rumors (USDJPY >160.7 or pace >1% in 2 days would likely trigger)
FOMC decision & Chair Warsh commentary June 18 (hawkish surprise would widen spread, test yen further; dovish would compress)
Iran deal signing June 19 and Hormuz reopening pace (if delayed or fails, oil/inflation pressure returns)
USDJPY 1M implied vol—currently ~8%; spike >12% would signal intervention fear or carry unwind starting
Nikkei 225 / TOPIX banks divergence from USDJPY (if equities sell off while yen weak, signals stress; today +5% bullish but watch reversal)
